aliases: Korean Fried Chicken with Carbonara Sauce

english: Korean Fried Chicken with Carbonara Sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for Korean boneless carbonara fried chicken; closest parent 'fried chicken food product' (FOODON_00002698) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (chicken meat, coated and fried) is the closest parent; no specific code for carbonara-sauced Korean fried chicken exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for Korean boneless carbonara fried chicken.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical restaurant serving of boneless carbonara chicken is approximately 300 g, comprising several pieces of crispy fried chicken coated in a creamy carbonara sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Fried chicken, battered or breaded, coated — adapted with carbonara sauce component; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy Item 05062 (Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter) and USDA FoodData Central Item 01009 (Cream sauce / carbonara sauce components)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct HPB Singapore or ICMR-NIN entry exists for Korean boneless carbonara fried chicken. Values estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for batter-fried chicken (USDA FDC #05062, ~250 kcal/100g, protein ~22g, fat ~14g, carb ~8g) with a carbonara cream sauce component (USDA FDC cream sauce analogue, ~100 kcal/100g, fat ~9g, protein ~2g, carb ~4g) at an approximate 70:30 chicken-to-sauce ratio by weight. Final per-100g values represent a blended estimate. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fried chicken and battered foods — analogous estimate based on battered/breaded fried chicken; University of Sydney GI Database reference for mixed protein-fat-carbohydrate fried foods
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Korean boneless carbonara fried chicken. GI estimated at 50 based on analogous battered fried chicken entries in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values in the range 40–55 for battered/breaded fried chicken products. The carbonara cream sauce component (high fat, low carbohydrate) further attenuates glycemic response. GI of 50 is a conservative mid-range estimate; gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (50 × 28.5) / 100 = 14.3, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 9.5 × 3 = 28.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1440 mg per 300 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish as a single 300 g serving provides approximately 1440 mg sodium, representing over 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive adults.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~14 per serving) and high total fat content from deep-frying and carbonara sauce
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish in small portions and infrequently, as the moderate glycemic load combined with high saturated fat from the carbonara cream sauce may impair postprandial glucose and insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~4.8 g/100 g), elevated sodium (~480 mg/100 g), and trans fat presence (~0.2 g/100 g) from deep-frying
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dish due to its combination of high saturated fat from the carbonara cream sauce, elevated sodium, and trace trans fats from deep-frying, all of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Boneless Carbonara Chicken is a Korean fried chicken dish consisting of deboned, batter-coated chicken pieces that are deep-fried until golden and crispy, then generously coated in a rich carbonara-style cream sauce made from cream, egg yolk, parmesan cheese, and black pepper. The dish is a fusion of Korean fried chicken (yangnyeom chicken culture) with Italian carbonara flavour profiles, popular in Korean fried chicken restaurants and chains across South Korea and in Korean restaurant outlets in Singapore. The texture contrasts a crunchy exterior with a creamy, savoury sauce coating. Nutritionally, the dish is calorie-dense (~220 kcal/100 g) with moderate protein (~16.5 g/100 g), high fat (~13.5 g/100 g including ~4.8 g saturated fat), and moderate carbohydrate (~9.5 g/100 g) from the batter. Sodium content is high (~480 mg/100 g), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 50 (low GI), but the glycemic load per typical 300 g serving is approximately 14, which is moderate. Clinically, this dish is best consumed occasionally and in controlled portions by individuals with metabolic or cardiovascular conditions. It contains common allergens including gluten, egg, milk, and soy.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Korean carbonara fried chicken?
Is carbonara chicken high in sodium?
Can diabetics eat Korean fried chicken with carbonara sauce?
What allergens are in carbonara fried chicken?
What is the GI of fried chicken with cream sauce?
How much protein in boneless carbonara chicken?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Korean boneless carbonara fried chicken was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values were estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for batter-fried chicken with a carbonara cream sauce component at an approximate 70:30 ratio. GI was estimated from analogous battered fried chicken entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category and the carbonara sauce component does not contribute a measurable amount in the available reference data. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy reference used for this estimation. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data used for this estimation. Trans fat is present at a low level (~0.2 g/100 g) due to deep-frying; this is a conservative estimate. The price of SGD 52.80 from the field survey source likely reflects a full set meal or family-size portion rather than a single individual serving; the serving size used for nutrient calculations is 300 g (individual serving). GI set to 50 based on analogous battered fried chicken from the University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (50 × 28.5) / 100 = 14.3 for a 300 g serving.

improvement_actions_taken: Canonical food name normalized from 'Boneless Carbonara Chicken (W)' to 'Boneless Carbonara Chicken'; original menu suffix '(W)' preserved in aliases.
Nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy using 70:30 chicken-to-sauce blending methodology as no direct approved-source entry exists.
GI estimated at 50 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous battered fried chicken entries; gi_category set to 'low' per ≤55 threshold.
Korean local-language name added to local_names.korean and aliases_by_language.korean per G6 mandatory Korean cuisine requirement.
All -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) documented in notes with source justification.
